statement.en: Diet of the Tawny Owl (Strix aluco) in non-breeding season in Východoslovenská pahorkatina Hills
statement.summary: In this study, we present the results of analyses of tawny owl (Strix aluco) pellets collected from three locations in the East Slovak Hills. The owls used daytime shelters in buildings (a stable attic and abandoned wine cellars) for varying periods between 2013 and 2024. In total, we identified 1,491 prey items in the food remains, including 21 species of mammals (Mammalia, 49.9%), 34 species of birds (Aves, 7.7%), and 2 species of amphibians (Amphibia, 1.0%). An exceptionally high proportion of the diet consisted of invertebrates (Evertebrata, 46.2%), particularly orthopterans (Orthoptera, 36.5%), with crickets (Gryllus sp.) being recorded in the pellets from the village of Priekopa from autumn 2014 to summer 2015. This represents an extraordinary dietary specialization of the tawny owl,

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Empirical ornithological study with direct observational data (pellet analysis) from 2013-2024 spanning 11 years across three field locations. Diet composition findings are reproducible through established zooarcheological methodology. Claims are falsifiable and grounded in specimen examination.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 無脊椎動物が脊椎動物の重みとほぼ等しくなる、捕食者の奇妙な均衡。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 同心円が回転する中で、外側の昆虫と内側の脊椎動物が互いに重さを占める。
